evals.name: greenfield-secure-release
evals.prompt: I need a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers when I push a tag. It should build my TypeScript project, generate a changelog using git-cliff, and create a GitHub release. Make it as secure as possible.
evals.expected_output: A .yml file with scoped permissions, SHA-pinned actions, and OIDC for releases.

evals.name: brownfield-hardening
evals.prompt:
Update this workflow to be secure: 
name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm install && npm test
evals.expected_output: Refactored workflow with specific permissions, SHA pinning, and Harden Runner integration.

evals.name: optimization-challenge
evals.prompt: Our CI is taking 10 minutes. Most of the time is spent in `npm install`. Can you optimize this workflow for speed while maintaining SLSA 3 compliance?
evals.expected_output: Workflow with pnpm/npm caching, parallel jobs, and provenance generation.
